statement: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun.
explanation: Mercury has the smallest average orbital distance from the Sun, at about 58 million kilometers. It completes one orbit in just 88 Earth days.

statement: The Sahara is the largest desert on Earth.
explanation: Antarctica is the largest desert overall because deserts are defined by low precipitation, not heat. The Sahara is the largest hot desert.

statement: Sound travels faster through water than through air.
explanation: Sound waves move much more quickly through water because its particles are packed more closely together. In seawater, sound travels at roughly 1,500 meters per second.

statement: An adult human body typically has 206 bones.
explanation: Most adults have 206 bones, though the exact number can vary slightly because some bones may fuse or remain separate.

statement: The Atlantic Ocean is the largest ocean on Earth.
explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean by surface area and volume. The Atlantic is the second largest.

statement: Venus is the hottest planet in the Solar System.
explanation: Although Mercury is closer to the Sun, Venus has a thick carbon-dioxide atmosphere that traps heat extremely efficiently through a runaway greenhouse effect.

statement: Bananas grow on trees.
explanation: Bananas grow on giant herbs, not trees. The 'trunk' is a pseudostem made from tightly packed leaf bases.

statement: The capital of Canada is Toronto.
explanation: Ottawa is the capital of Canada. Toronto is the most populous city in the country.

statement: Glass is made primarily from sand.
explanation: Most ordinary glass is made mainly from silica, which is found in sand. Other ingredients are added to change its melting point and properties.

statement: Water expands when it freezes.
explanation: Ice forms an open crystal structure that takes up more space than liquid water. That is why ice floats.

statement: Australia lies entirely in the Northern Hemisphere.
explanation: Australia is entirely in the Southern Hemisphere. The Tropic of Capricorn passes through the country.

statement: An octopus has three hearts.
explanation: Two hearts pump blood to the gills, while a third pumps blood to the rest of the body.

statement: Light travels faster than sound.
explanation: In a vacuum, light travels at about 300,000 kilometers per second. Sound is far slower because it needs a medium such as air or water.

statement: The chemical symbol for silver is Ag.
explanation: Ag comes from the Latin word argentum. Many chemical symbols are based on older Latin names.

statement: Koalas are a type of bear.
explanation: Koalas are marsupials, not bears. They carry their young in a pouch.

statement: A leap year occurs every four years without exception.
explanation: Century years are not leap years unless they are divisible by 400. For example, 1900 was not a leap year, but 2000 was.

statement: Machu Picchu is in Peru.
explanation: Machu Picchu is a famous Inca site high in the Andes Mountains of Peru.

statement: Human skin is the body's largest organ.
explanation: Skin covers the entire body and plays a major role in protection, sensation, and temperature regulation.

statement: Penguins live only in the Arctic.
explanation: Penguins are native to the Southern Hemisphere. None are naturally found in the Arctic.

statement: The Suez Canal connects the Mediterranean Sea and the Red Sea.
explanation: The canal cuts across Egypt and provides a direct shipping route between Europe and Asia without rounding Africa.

statement: Bamboo is a type of grass.
explanation: Despite its woody appearance, bamboo belongs to the grass family Poaceae.

statement: Jupiter has more than 70 known moons.
explanation: Jupiter has well over 70 known moons and the total continues to change as new moons are confirmed.

statement: The Roman numeral L means 100.
explanation: L represents 50. The Roman numeral for 100 is C.

statement: Caffeine occurs naturally in tea leaves.
explanation: Tea plants naturally produce caffeine, just like coffee plants do.

statement: The Pacific Ring of Fire is mainly associated with earthquakes and volcanoes.
explanation: It follows the edges of several tectonic plates around the Pacific Ocean, which makes it one of the most geologically active regions on Earth.

statement: Saturn is the only planet in the Solar System with rings.
explanation: Jupiter, Uranus, and Neptune also have ring systems. Saturn's are simply the most spectacular and easiest to see.

statement: The blue whale is the largest animal known to have lived.
explanation: Blue whales exceed even the largest known dinosaurs in overall mass.

statement: The currency used in Japan is the yen.
explanation: Japan's national currency is the yen, abbreviated as JPY in international finance.

statement: Shakespeare wrote The Odyssey.
explanation: The Odyssey is an ancient Greek epic poem traditionally attributed to Homer.

statement: The Great Barrier Reef is off the coast of Queensland.
explanation: It stretches along the northeastern coast of Australia, mostly off the state of Queensland.

statement: An ostrich's eye is larger than its brain.
explanation: Ostriches have exceptionally large eyes, each bigger than their relatively small brain.

statement: Humans have exactly five senses and no more.
explanation: Beyond sight, hearing, smell, taste, and touch, humans also have senses such as balance, pain, temperature, and body position.

statement: Mount Kilimanjaro is in Kenya.
explanation: Mount Kilimanjaro is in Tanzania, near the Kenyan border.

statement: Pure honey can remain edible for years.
explanation: Honey's low moisture and acidic chemistry make it very resistant to spoilage when it is stored properly.

statement: The Nile flows into the Mediterranean Sea.
explanation: The Nile runs north through northeastern Africa and empties into the Mediterranean in Egypt.

statement: The human heart sits exactly in the center of the chest.
explanation: The heart sits slightly left of center, although much of it remains behind the breastbone.

statement: A standard piano has 88 keys.
explanation: Most modern full-size pianos have 52 white keys and 36 black keys, for a total of 88.

statement: Mandarin Chinese has more native speakers than English.
explanation: English is dominant globally as a second language, but Mandarin has more first-language speakers.

statement: Gold is denser than silver.
explanation: Gold is significantly denser than silver, which is why equal-sized pieces of gold weigh more.

statement: The Moon produces its own light.
explanation: Moonlight is reflected sunlight. The Moon does not generate visible light of its own.

statement: Mario first appeared in the arcade game Donkey Kong.
explanation: Nintendo first introduced the character later known as Mario in the 1981 arcade game Donkey Kong.

statement: The original Tetris was created in Japan.
explanation: Tetris was designed in 1984 by Alexey Pajitnov in the Soviet Union, not in Japan.

statement: Valve developed the original Half-Life.
explanation: Half-Life was Valve's debut game and was released in 1998.

statement: In most Legend of Zelda games, the main playable hero is named Zelda.
explanation: The playable hero is usually Link. Zelda is the princess after whom the series is named.

statement: The Nintendo Wii launched before the Nintendo Switch.
explanation: The Wii launched in 2006, while the Nintendo Switch launched in 2017.

statement: The Premier League was founded in 1992.
explanation: England's top division broke away from the Football League in 1992, with the first Premier League season beginning in 1992-93.

statement: Anfield is the home stadium of Manchester United.
explanation: Anfield is Liverpool's home ground. Manchester United play at Old Trafford.

statement: Arsenal are nicknamed the Gunners.
explanation: Arsenal's long-standing nickname is the Gunners, a reference to the club's roots in Woolwich's armaments industry.

statement: A win in the Premier League is worth two points.
explanation: A win is worth three points, a draw is worth one, and a loss is worth none.

statement: Chelsea play their home league matches at Stamford Bridge.
explanation: Chelsea's home stadium is Stamford Bridge in west London.

statement: The 2020 Trackmania reboot ("Trackmania", by Nadeo / Ubisoft Nadeo) ships with only the Stadium environment, like TMNF before it.
explanation: Stadium only — Bay, Coast, Island and the other Trackmania environments are not part of the 2020 reboot.

statement: The Cup of the Day (COTD) in Trackmania (2020) is decided in a 64-player head-to-head knockout bracket after an open qualifier.
explanation: The top 64 from the daily qualifier advance into a single-elimination knockout to win the cup.

statement: Ubisoft acquired Nadeo in 2009.
explanation: Ubisoft acquired the French studio behind Trackmania in 2009.

statement: The official Trackmania World Tour finals were held at the Accor Arena in Paris in 2022, in front of around 15,000 spectators.
explanation: The ZrT Trackmania Cup final filled the Accor Arena in 2022 — widely treated as the headline live event of the year.

statement: Age of Empires II: The Age of Kings was originally released by Ensemble Studios in 1999.
explanation: Released in September 1999 by Ensemble Studios, published by Microsoft.

statement: In Age of Empires II, the Trebuchet first becomes available in the Castle Age.
explanation: The Trebuchet is an Imperial-Age unit — castles can train them only after the Imperial Age upgrade.

statement: William Wallace serves as the in-game learning campaign in Age of Empires II.
explanation: The Wallace campaign is the official tutorial for The Age of Kings.

statement: The Aztecs were a playable civilisation in Age of Empires II: The Age of Kings on launch in 1999.
explanation: Aztecs were added with The Conquerors expansion in 2000.

statement: Age of Empires IV was developed by Relic Entertainment and released in 2021.
explanation: Relic Entertainment developed AoE IV, released October 2021.

statement: The "wololo" chant in Age of Empires belongs to the Priest / Monk unit converting enemies.
explanation: The Monk's conversion ability is famously voiced as "wololo".

statement: Charmander evolves directly into Charizard.
explanation: Charmander → Charmeleon → Charizard. Charmeleon is the middle stage.

statement: Mew, the 151st Pokémon, was famously hidden in Pokémon Red and Blue by Game Freak programmer Shigeki Morimoto after the game had already gone gold.
explanation: Morimoto squeezed Mew into the ROM with no official approval; it was discovered later by players and event distributors.

statement: Pokémon Gold and Silver were released worldwide in 1999.
explanation: Japan only in 1999. North America in October 2000; Europe in 2001.

statement: Eevee can evolve into more different Pokémon than any other Pokémon in the franchise.
explanation: Eight different evolutions across the series (the "Eeveelutions") — more than any other line.

statement: Pokémon Scarlet and Violet introduced the Paldea region.
explanation: Released November 2022 — Paldea is the Iberian-inspired ninth-generation region.

statement: The three "Egyptian God" cards in Yu-Gi-Oh! are Slifer the Sky Dragon, Obelisk the Tormentor, and The Winged Dragon of Ra.
explanation: These are the three God cards from the original Battle City arc.

statement: Seto Kaiba's signature monster is the Red-Eyes Black Dragon.
explanation: Kaiba's signature is Blue-Eyes White Dragon. Red-Eyes Black Dragon belongs to Joey Wheeler (Jonouchi Katsuya).

statement: Yu-Gi-Oh! was originally a manga by Kazuki Takahashi serialised in Weekly Shōnen Jump.
explanation: Takahashi's manga ran in Weekly Shōnen Jump from 1996 to 2004.

statement: In the Trading Card Game, Dark Magician's original Attack stat is 2500.
explanation: 2500 ATK / 2100 DEF — Yugi's signature monster.

statement: Cabal Online is a Korean MMORPG developed by ESTsoft, first released in 2005.
explanation: Korean release was in October 2005; global service expanded over the following years.

statement: At Cabal Online's original 2005 launch, six classes were playable.
explanation: Warrior, Blader, Wizard, Force Archer, Force Shielder, Force Blader were the original six.

statement: Cabal Online's Force Gunner class was introduced in 2014.
explanation: Force Gunner was the first class added after the original six, debuting in 2014.

statement: World of Warcraft was first released in North America on 23 November 2004.
explanation: 23 November 2004 — WoW's North American launch date.

statement: The Burning Crusade introduced the Draenei to the Alliance and the Blood Elves to the Horde.
explanation: TBC (2007) added both new races simultaneously, one per faction.

statement: At launch in 2004, World of Warcraft shipped with nine playable classes.
explanation: Druid, Hunter, Mage, Paladin, Priest, Rogue, Shaman, Warlock, Warrior.

statement: The Death Knight was the first "hero class" added to WoW, in Wrath of the Lich King (2008).
explanation: The Death Knight launched as WoW's first hero class with WotLK in 2008.

statement: The maximum raid size in vanilla WoW was forty players.
explanation: 40-man raids: Molten Core, Onyxia's Lair, Blackwing Lair, AQ40, Naxxramas.

statement: Kékestető, in the Mátra range, is Hungary's highest point at 1,014 metres.
explanation: Kékestető — 1,014 m above sea level — is the highest peak in Hungary.

statement: The Tisza flows for a longer stretch inside Hungary's borders than the Danube does.
explanation: Tisza ~597 km in-country versus Danube ~417 km.

statement: The Hortobágy National Park was Hungary's first national park, founded in 1973.
explanation: Hortobágy was Hungary's first national park — established 1 January 1973.

statement: Lake Velence is larger than Lake Balaton.
explanation: Balaton (~594 km²) is about 24× the surface area of Lake Velence (~26 km²).

statement: Aggtelek and the Baradla cave system are a UNESCO World Heritage Site shared with Slovakia.
explanation: Joint listing as "Caves of Aggtelek Karst and Slovak Karst" since 1995.

statement: Saul fia (Son of Saul, 2015), directed by László Nemes, won the Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film.
explanation: Won at the 88th Academy Awards ceremony in 2016.

statement: István Szabó's Mephisto (1981) was the first Hungarian film to win the Best Foreign Language Film Oscar.
explanation: Mephisto took the Foreign Language Film Oscar at the 1982 ceremony — the first Hungarian winner.

statement: Omega's signature ballad "Gyöngyhajú lány" was originally released in 1969.
explanation: Released in 1969 on Omega's second studio album.

statement: Hungarian rock band Tankcsapda formed in Budapest in 1985.
explanation: Tankcsapda formed in Debrecen in 1989, not Budapest in 1985.

statement: "LGT" stands for Locomotiv GT.
explanation: Locomotiv GT — the iconic Hungarian rock band formed in 1971.

statement: Béla Bartók and Zoltán Kodály jointly devised a system of Hungarian folk-song collection at the start of the 20th century.
explanation: Bartók and Kodály began their joint folk-music collection expeditions in 1905-06.

statement: Quimby's song "Most múlik pontosan" was originally written and recorded by another Hungarian artist before Quimby covered it.
explanation: It is a Quimby original, off the 2003 album Ékszerelmére.

statement: The 2017 Cannes-winning Hungarian film "Testről és lélekről" (On Body and Soul) was directed by Ildikó Enyedi.
explanation: Won the Golden Bear at Berlin in 2017 and was nominated for the Best Foreign Language Film Oscar.

statement: The French Bulldog has been the American Kennel Club's most-registered breed in the United States every year since 2022.
explanation: Frenchies overtook the Labrador in 2022 (AKC registration data) and have held the top spot since.

statement: Dachshunds were originally bred to hunt badgers.
explanation: "Dachs" = badger in German. Their long bodies and short legs were bred for going underground after them.

statement: A Komondor's distinctive corded coat is fully formed by the age of two months.
explanation: Cords take roughly two years to fully develop — puppies start out fluffy.

statement: The Hungarian Vizsla and the Wirehaired Vizsla are recognised as two separate FCI breeds.
explanation: Magyar Vizsla (short-haired) and Drótszőrű Magyar Vizsla (wirehaired) are listed as distinct FCI breeds.

statement: Border Collies were ranked the most intelligent dog breed by Stanley Coren's The Intelligence of Dogs (1994).
explanation: Coren's ranking placed Border Collies at #1 for working/obedience intelligence.

statement: Valve released the Steam Deck in February 2022.
explanation: The original Steam Deck shipped to early reservers on 25 February 2022.

statement: Microsoft's acquisition of Activision Blizzard closed in October 2023.
explanation: Closed on 13 October 2023 after the UK CMA approved a restructured deal.

statement: The first "Souls-like" game by FromSoftware was Dark Souls (2011).
explanation: Demon's Souls (2009) preceded Dark Souls and is the original of the lineage.

statement: Baldur's Gate 3 won The Game Awards "Game of the Year" in 2023.
explanation: Larian Studios' Baldur's Gate 3 took GOTY at TGA 2023.

statement: Elden Ring had sold over 25 million copies worldwide by the end of 2024.
explanation: FromSoftware/Bandai Namco reported 25+ million units by mid-2024 and 28+ million by early 2025.

statement: Pálinka is an EU-protected geographical designation: only Hungarian-made fruit spirits (and apricot pálinka from four Austrian states) may use the name commercially.
explanation: Pálinka has EU PDO status restricting commercial use of the name.

statement: Unicum has been produced by the Zwack family since 1790.
explanation: The Zwack family has produced Unicum, the iconic Hungarian herbal liqueur, since 1790.

statement: Tokaji Aszú is made primarily from grapes affected by noble rot (Botrytis cinerea).
explanation: The noble-rot-shrivelled aszú berries give Tokaji Aszú its concentrated sweetness.

statement: Louis XIV of France famously called Tokaji Aszú "the wine of devils."
explanation: He called it "vinum regum, rex vinorum" — "the wine of kings, the king of wines."

statement: Egri Bikavér ("Bull's Blood of Eger") must be a red wine blend, not a single varietal.
explanation: Hungarian wine law defines Egri Bikavér as a cuvée of at least three permitted varieties.

statement: Marco Rossi has been head coach of the Hungarian men's national football team since 2018.
explanation: Appointed in June 2018 and still in charge for the 2024-25 cycle and beyond.

statement: Hungary held France to a 1–1 draw at Euro 2020, in Budapest.
explanation: Group F, 19 June 2021 at the Puskás Aréna — Attila Fiola opened the scoring before Griezmann equalised.

statement: Dominik Szoboszlai scored a late winner against Iceland in the Euro 2020 playoff final.
explanation: November 2020 — Szoboszlai's late strike booked Hungary's Euro 2020 ticket.

statement: Hungary has qualified for three consecutive UEFA European Championships: 2016, 2020 and 2024.
explanation: Hungary qualified for Euro 2016 (first time since 1972), Euro 2020 and Euro 2024 in succession.

statement: Roland Sallai joined the German Bundesliga club SC Freiburg in 2018.
explanation: Sallai moved to Freiburg in 2018 from APOEL Nicosia.

statement: Cristiano Ronaldo is the all-time top scorer in the UEFA Champions League / European Cup.
explanation: Ronaldo sits at the top of the all-time scoring chart with ~140 goals.

statement: Paris Saint-Germain won their first ever European Cup / UEFA Champions League in the 2024-25 season, beating Inter Milan 5–0 in the final.
explanation: Final played 31 May 2025 at the Allianz Arena, Munich.

statement: The UEFA Champions League anthem was composed by Hans Zimmer.
explanation: Composed by Tony Britten in 1992, inspired by Handel's "Zadok the Priest".

statement: The 2024-25 season was the first to use a single 36-team "league phase" replacing the traditional group stage.
explanation: UEFA replaced the eight-group format with a Swiss-model 36-team league phase from 2024-25.

statement: No club has ever won the European Cup / Champions League three years in a row except Real Madrid.
explanation: Real Madrid have done it twice — the original five-in-a-row (1956–1960) and the 2016/17/18 three-peat.
